findings.title: phraseBoost literal 0.25 is an undocumented magic number
findings.description: The constant 0.25 added to similarity on a verbatim phrase match has no explanation. The scoring formula blends a token-coverage ratio (0–1.0) with an ad-hoc additive boost whose origin is opaque. A future maintainer cannot tell whether this was empirically tuned or guessed. It also silently allows similarity > 1.0 (perfect token match + phrase boost = 1.25), which could surprise callers that assume a normalised score.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract to a named module-level constant: `const PHRASE_MATCH_BOOST = 0.25 // additive bonus for verbatim phrase hit`. Add a comment if the value was empirically calibrated.

findings.title: Top-K limit 5 is silently duplicated across both retrieval paths
findings.description: The semantic path hardcodes 5 in `vectorStore.search(Array.from(queryEmbedding), 5)` (line 79, pre-existing) and the new text-search fallback re-hardcodes the same limit via `limit = 5` (line 103). Both represent the concept of max retrieved notes but will drift independently if either is changed. The fallback's default parameter also hides the coupling from the call site.
evidence: async function retrieveRelevantNotesByText(query: string, limit = 5): Promise<RetrievedNote[]>
findings.suggested_fix: Declare `const MAX_RETRIEVED_NOTES = 5` at module scope and replace both literals. Pass it explicitly at the fallback call site: `return retrieveRelevantNotesByText(cleanQuery, MAX_RETRIEVED_NOTES)`.

findings.title: `tokenize` is a generic text utility living in a domain module
findings.description: `tokenize` (lowercase alphanumeric token extraction from a string) has zero dependency on notes, embeddings, or RAG semantics. `textUtils.ts` is already imported in this file and houses analogous generic helpers (`stripHtml`, `getInitials`). Keeping `tokenize` in `noteQA.ts` makes it invisible to future search-adjacent callers and risks independent reimplementation elsewhere.
evidence: function tokenize(value: string): string[] { return value.toLowerCase().match(/[a-z0-9]+/g) ?? [] }
findings.suggested_fix: Move to `src/lib/textUtils.ts` and export it. Import here alongside the existing `stripHtml` import. Zero behaviour change.
